summary: This week's AI news highlights significant developments in AI regulations, advancements in AI technology, and growing concerns over AI safety and ethics. Major companies like Google, Apple, and NVIDIA are making strides in AI innovation, while governments and organizations are working to establish guidelines and standards for AI use.
insight: The increasing use of AI in various industries is leading to a rise in AI-related incidents, emphasizing the need for robust security measures and regulations to ensure responsible AI development and deployment.
highlights: Anthropic's $1.5B copyright settlement has been approved, setting a precedent for AI copyright issues.
Google is working on a new AI chip to improve the efficiency of its Gemini models.
China's AI companies are gaining ground, with Moonshot and Alibaba unveiling models that rival those of OpenAI and Anthropic.

articles.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
articles.description: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

articles.title: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
articles.description: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.

articles.title: OpenAI is scared of open-weight models. Should the US be?
articles.description: Talk of banning Chinese-made open-weight LLMs reveals the challenge of  turning AI into a business.

articles.title: China’s AI models have Trump’s AI world at war with itself

articles.title: Safety and alignment in an era of long-horizon models
articles.description: OpenAI shares lessons from deploying long-running AI models, highlighting new safety risks, observed failures, and improved safeguards through iterative deployment.

articles.title: Can an Apple lawsuit derail OpenAI’s hardware plans?
articles.description: On the latest episode of Equity, we debate whether Apple's lawsuit will cast a shadow over OpenAi's much-discussed plans to get into hardware and go public.

articles.title: Nonprofit Current AI is racing to build the World Wide Web of AI, free for all
articles.description: Current AI, a nonprofit building AI that leaves no one culture behind, has made remarkable progress across devices, AI chat, and more.

articles.title: NVIDIA Vera Rubin Maximizes Intelligence per Dollar for Post-Training Workloads — a Key Metric for Agentic AI
articles.description: Lowest cost per token from extreme codesign maximizes intelligence per dollar for post-training in the agentic era.

articles.title: It's official: EU will force Google to share search data and open up AI on Android
articles.description: Google says these changes could endanger user privacy and security.
